column2: Local hostname/device that emitted the log; scopes events and supports cross-host correlation.
Emitting service name (e.g., sshd, sudo, su); anchors authentication/authorization pathway alignment.
Process identifier when available (e.g., sshd[1234]); links multi-line auth flows within a process.
Canonical auth event category inferred from template (e.g., ssh_login, sudo_cmd, pam_session).
Template action verb (e.g., accepted/failed/opened/closed); aligns lifecycle steps across logs.
Primary user referenced by the event (session user or login target); not always the initiator.
Initiating user explicitly stated (e.g., 'su ... by X'); aligns who triggered the action.
Recorded caller of an action (e.g., 'sudo: X'); distinguishes the invoker from the target identity.
Privilege/identity target of the action (e.g., sudo USER=root, su for Y); aligns what role was assumed.
Auth/session handle (e.g., systemd-logind session or PAM session marker); ties open/close pairs.
Terminal identifier (pts/N or unknown); separates interactive vs non-interactive execution contexts.
Working directory context (from PWD); aligns with audit cwd for provenance correlation.
Executed command string (e.g., sudo COMMAND=...); primary payload for behavior alignment.
Remote source IP for network authentication (sshd); aligns ingress origin with audit src_ip.
Remote source port (sshd); disambiguates concurrent connections from the same src_ip.
PAM service domain (sshd/sudo/su/cron); distinguishes authentication domains and session boundaries.
PAM action/phase outcome (opened/closed/auth); refines session lifecycle alignment.
Derived auth outcome when possible (success/failure/unknown); normalizes results across sources.
